core.database.base module

DATABASE INTERFACE – Abstract Base Class for All Database Adapters

PURPOSE:

Defines a common interface that all database adapters (SQLite, PostgreSQL) must implement. This allows switching databases by simply changing the config file, without modifying any application code.

WHY THIS EXISTS:

  • To support future migration from SQLite to PostgreSQL

  • To keep database code modular and testable

  • To enforce consistent method signatures across adapters

  • To implement the adapter pattern for database flexibility

UNITS AND ASSUMPTIONS:

  • All time values are in nanoseconds

  • All energy values are in microjoules

  • All methods raise DatabaseError on failures

  • Context manager support for transactions

AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy

exception core.database.base.DatabaseError[source]

Bases: Exception

Base exception for all database operations.

class core.database.base.DatabaseInterface[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for all database adapters.

Any new database adapter (SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL) must implement all methods defined here. This ensures the rest of the application can interact with any database seamlessly.

The interface is divided into sections: 1. Connection management 2. Transaction handling 3. Table creation 4. Core data insertion 5. Query methods 6. Context manager support

abstractmethod close() → None[source]

Close database connection and release resources.

This method should: - Close the connection gracefully - Release any connection pool resources - Be safe to call multiple times

abstractmethod commit() → None[source]

Commit the current transaction.

abstractmethod connect() → None[source]

Establish connection to the database.

This method should: - Create the connection using config parameters - Set any necessary pragmas (for SQLite) - Configure connection pool (for PostgreSQL) - Raise DatabaseError if connection fails

Raises:

DatabaseError – If connection fails

abstractmethod create_tables() → None[source]

Create all necessary tables if they don’t exist.

For SQLite, this runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements. For PostgreSQL, this may use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS or migrations.

Tables to create: - experiments - hardware_config - idle_baselines - runs - orchestration_events - orchestration_tax_summary - energy_samples - cpu_samples - interrupt_samples

Also creates indexes and views (ml_features).

abstractmethod create_tax_summaries(exp_id: int) → None[source]

Create tax summary entries for all agentic runs in an experiment.

This method: 1. Finds all runs in the experiment 2. Pairs linear and agentic runs by run_number 3. Computes tax = agentic_dynamic_uj - linear_dynamic_uj 4. Stores results in orchestration_tax_summary table

Parameters:

exp_id – Experiment ID

abstractmethod execute(query: str, params: tuple | dict | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Execute a raw SQL query and return results.

Parameters:

  • query – SQL query string with placeholders

  • params – Parameters for the query (tuple for positional, dict for named)

Returns:

List of dictionaries, each representing a row with column names as keys

Raises:

DatabaseError – If query execution fails

Example

results = db.execute(

“SELECT * FROM runs WHERE exp_id = ?”, (exp_id,)

)

abstractmethod execute_many(query: str, params_list: List[tuple | dict]) → int[source]

Execute the same query with multiple parameter sets.

Used for batch inserts where performance matters.

Parameters:

  • query – SQL query string

  • params_list – List of parameter sets, each a tuple or dict

Returns:

Number of rows affected

Raises:

DatabaseError – If execution fails

abstractmethod get_ml_data(workflow: str | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve flattened ML data from the ml_features view.

This is the primary method for loading data into ML pipelines. The view contains all features and targets in a single row per run.

Parameters:

workflow – If provided, filter by ‘linear’ or ‘agentic’

Returns:

List of dictionaries, each representing one run with all ML columns

abstractmethod get_run(run_id: int) → Dict[str, Any] | None[source]

Retrieve a single run by its ID.

abstractmethod get_runs_by_experiment(exp_id: int) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve all runs for an experiment, ordered by run_number.

abstractmethod get_tax_summaries(exp_id: int | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Retrieve tax summaries, optionally filtered by experiment.

Parameters:

exp_id – If provided, only summaries for runs in that experiment

Returns:

List of tax summary dictionaries

abstractmethod insert_baseline(baseline_data: Dict[str, Any]) → str[source]

Insert an idle baseline measurement.

Parameters:

baseline_data – Dictionary with keys: - baseline_id: str (optional, generated if not provided) - timestamp: float - package_power_watts: float - core_power_watts: float - uncore_power_watts: float (optional) - dram_power_watts: float (optional) - duration_seconds: int - sample_count: int - package_std: float (optional) - core_std: float (optional) - governor: str - turbo: str (‘enabled’/’disabled’) - background_cpu: float - process_count: int - method: str

Returns:

baseline_id of the new baseline

abstractmethod insert_cpu_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert CPU samples.

Parameters:

  • run_id – Foreign key to runs table

  • samples – List of sample dictionaries with keys: - timestamp_ns: int - cpu_util_percent: float - cpu_busy_mhz: float - cpu_avg_mhz: float - c1_residency: float - c2_residency: float - c3_residency: float - c6_residency: float - c7_residency: float - pkg_c8_residency: float - pkg_c9_residency: float - pkg_c10_residency: float - package_power: float - dram_power: float - gpu_rc6: float - package_temp: float - ipc: float - extra_metrics_json: str (optional)

abstractmethod insert_energy_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert high-frequency RAPL samples.

Parameters:

  • run_id – Foreign key to runs table

  • samples – List of sample dictionaries with keys: - timestamp_ns: int - pkg_energy_uj: int (optional) - core_energy_uj: int (optional) - uncore_energy_uj: int (optional) - dram_energy_uj: int (optional)

abstractmethod insert_experiment(experiment_data: Dict[str, Any]) → int[source]

Insert a new experiment record.

Parameters:

experiment_data – Dictionary with keys: - name: str - Experiment name - description: str (optional) - Description - workflow_type: str - ‘linear’ or ‘agentic’ - model_name: str - LLM model identifier - provider: str - ‘cloud’ or ‘local’ - task_name: str - Task identifier - country_code: str - ISO country code

Returns:

exp_id of the newly created experiment

abstractmethod insert_hardware(hardware_data: Dict[str, Any]) → int[source]

Insert or retrieve hardware configuration.

Should check for existing record (by hostname + kernel_version) and return existing hw_id if found.

Parameters:

hardware_data – Dictionary with keys: - hostname: str - cpu_model: str - cpu_cores: int - cpu_threads: int - ram_gb: int - kernel_version: str - microcode_version: str (optional) - rapl_domains: str (optional)

Returns:

hw_id of the hardware record (existing or new)

abstractmethod insert_interrupt_samples(run_id: int, samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert interrupt rate samples.

Parameters:

  • run_id – Foreign key to runs table

  • samples – List of sample dictionaries with keys: - timestamp_ns: int - interrupts_per_sec: float

abstractmethod insert_orchestration_events(run_id: int, events: List[Dict[str, Any]]) → None[source]

Insert orchestration events for a run.

Each event represents a phase (planning, execution, waiting, synthesis) or a specific action (LLM call, tool call).

Parameters:

  • run_id – Foreign key to runs table

  • events – List of event dictionaries with keys: - step_index: int (optional) - phase: str (‘planning’/’execution’/’waiting’/’synthesis’) - event_type: str - start_time_ns: int - end_time_ns: int - duration_ns: int - power_watts: float (optional) - cpu_util_percent: float (optional) - interrupt_rate: float (optional) - event_energy_uj: int - tax_contribution_uj: int - tax_percent: float

abstractmethod insert_run(run_data: Dict[str, Any]) → int[source]

Insert a single run with all its metrics.

This is the main method for inserting experiment results. It should extract all fields from the harness output and compute derived metrics.

Parameters:

run_data – Complete run dictionary from harness containing: - ml_features: dict with 77+ fields - layer3_derived: dict (optional) - execution: dict (optional) - sustainability: dict (optional) - orchestration_events: list (optional) - energy_samples: list (optional) - cpu_samples: list (optional) - interrupt_samples: list (optional)

Returns:

run_id of the newly inserted run

abstractmethod rollback() → None[source]

Rollback the current transaction.

abstractmethod transaction() → DatabaseInterface[source]

Begin a transaction. Returns self for context manager usage.

Usage:
with db.transaction():

db.insert_run(data1) db.insert_run(data2)

This ensures atomicity: both inserts succeed or both fail.
